DISCOVERY 612, NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM AND SPECIALLY PREPARED INTERROGATORIES, REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS, AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY NOS. 2.11, 12.1, 12.3, 12.4, 13.1, 13.2, 14.1, AND 50.1-50.6. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE DEFINITION OF INCIDENT IS ARGUMENTATIVE AND RENDERS THE INTERROGATORIES UNINTELLIGIBLE. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NOS. 1 AND 2. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE DEFINITION OF INCIDENT IS ARGUMENTATIVE AND RENDERS THE INTERROGATORIES UNINTELLIGIBLE. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NOS. 113, 14, AND 15 AS IRRELEVANT AND AS IT SEEKS WORK PRODUCT. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NOS. 14 AMD 15. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO REQUEST FOR DOCUMENTS NO. 3. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE DEFINITION OF INCIDENT IS ARGUMENTATIVE AND RENDERS THE INTERROGATORIES UNINTELLIGIBLE. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO REQUEST FOR DOCUMENTS NO. 8. THE RESPONSE IS NOT PROPERLY LIMITED. FURTHER RESPONSES SHALL BE MADE WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
